title: Delete Account Button with Soft Delete and User Blocking
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want to delete my account from the settings page, so that I can remove my data and revoke app access when I no longer need the service.
description:
Add a Delete Account feature to the settings page with a confirmation modal and soft-delete mechanism.

## User Flow
1. User clicks "Delete My Account" button on settings page
2. Confirmation modal: "Are you sure you would like to delete your account? Clicking Delete My Account will remove all data and metadata from this app."
3. Red delete button + cancel button in modal
4. On confirm: soft-delete the user (set `DeleteAccount` flag to true), remove Salesforce token and user details, then log user out
5. If deleted user attempts to re-authenticate: block access and display "Your account has been deleted. Please contact support if you want your account re-instated or believe you have received this message in error."
6. Fire a Zapier webhook with the same user fields as the signup event

## Technical Details
- Soft delete via `DeleteAccount` column in users table (not hard delete)
- Delete Salesforce token and email but retain user record
- Zapier webhook: `https://hooks.zapier.com/hooks/catch/2202974/3mjf292/`
- Blocked users see error message with contact link on login page
acceptance_criteria: Delete My Account button appears on settings page
Confirmation modal shows with red delete and cancel buttons
Cancel button closes modal without side effects
Confirm sets DeleteAccount flag to true in the database
Salesforce token and user details are cleared
User is logged out after account deletion
Deleted user is blocked from re-authenticating
Blocked user sees deletion message with support contact link
Zapier webhook fires with user data on deletion
Contact link in error message points to datatoolspro.com/contact
ux_changes: New Delete Account button on settings page with confirmation modal. Blocked users see an informative error message with support contact link on the login page.
middleware_changes: Soft-delete logic that sets the DeleteAccount flag, clears tokens, logs user out, fires Zapier webhook, and blocks re-authentication attempts.
data_model_changes: New `DeleteAccount` boolean column in the users table to flag soft-deleted accounts.
implementation_notes: Soft delete approach chosen for beta phase — allows manual review and data purge process later. Zapier webhook uses the same endpoint as user signup for consistent event tracking. Cancel button had a loading state bug that was fixed. Salesforce re-authorization screen should be shown again if a deleted user somehow re-connects.
comment_summary: Discussion about soft delete vs full delete approach. Developer implemented the DeleteAccount flag, blocking logic, and Zapier webhook. A bug where the cancel button got stuck on loading was identified and fixed. Error message was refined to include a contact support link.
